name: Melba Liston
description: American jazz trombonist, musical arranger, and composer (1926–1999)
abstract: Melba Doretta Liston (January 13, 1926 – April 23, 1999) was an American jazz trombonist, arranger, and composer. She was the first woman trombonist to play alongside men in big bands during the 1940s and 1960s, but as her career progressed she became better known as an arranger, particularly in partnership with pianist Randy Weston.

interviews.title: Melba Liston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
Kool Jazz Festival: Women Blow Their Own Horns

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: The trombone section was a very, very good section with Åke Persson playing lead, Melba Liston, Curtis Fuller, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: Melba Liston was the musical director... It was Melba, and Chuck Connors... She was the one that was the main arranger in the band... you did stuff with Melba Liston
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: Melba was also in Dizzy’s bandwidth me... I love every moment that I had with Melba... Oh yeah, that was a beautiful relationship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Melba Liston was with the band. She was writing and playing her trombone with the band. That's where I first met all of that gang

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: And then they had Melba Liston, they had, um Matthew Gee, another trombone player. ... And myself, that was the trumpet section
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: And the band that I joined was the band with Lee Morgan, Benny Golson, Wynton Kelly, Melba Liston was the musical director
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003
